raw.networking: The TECHNOLOGY PARTNERS Foundation is a member of such international organisations and associations as: EARTO – the European Association of Research and Technology Organisations (also a Board member) and K4I - Knowledge4Innovation (as a founding member and Board member).
raw.info_members: Participation in the Knowledge4Innovation platform.
raw.main_category_title: IV - Think tanks, research and academic institutions
raw.activity_eu_legislative: Through its participation in the European Association of Research and Technology Organisations (EARTO) and KNowledge4Innovation (K4I), the TECHNOLOGY PARTNERS Foundation is active in the area of formulating the bases for EU research and innovation policy making.
raw.activity_relevant_comm:
In 2018, as a member of a consortium led by the Technopolis Group, TPF participated in the Social Business Initiative (SBI) follow-up: Cooperation between social economy enterprises and traditional enterprises project, EASME/COSME/2016/018. The study aimed to deepen knowledge about cross-sector cooperation relationships between social enterprises and traditional profit-making enterprises and how to strengthen European social enterprises’ ecosystems. 
TPF contributed to the development of important initiatives aimed at strengthening the competitiveness and innovativeness of European science and economy, of which launching an initiative in the area of transport - the "Towards a single and innovative European Transport System” project, proposed by Prof. Jerzy Buzek, former President of the European Parliament – is an example. The initiatives were discussed during sessions co-organised by the Technology Partners Foundation as part of the programmes of the annual European Innovation Summits in the European Parliament in Brussels – see the list below. TPF took part in the SINTRAS („Towards a Single and Innovative European Transport System”) project (no. MOVE/C2/SER/2015-582/SI2.727958), commissioned by DG MOVE, coordinated by the Joint Institute for Innovation Policy (JIIP) and with the participation Rupprecht Consult – Forschung & Beratung GMBH. The main objective of the project, executed March-December 2016, was to recommend a set of implementable and effective actions to substantially improve Europe’s transport system through better adoption of innovation and new technology. To achieve this objective, the main tasks of the study included an in-depth examination of the current state of play in each focus area in each of the Member States; identification and description of the barriers and their underlying root causess and building roadmaps of policy interventions and actions, with initial monitoring indicators, targeting the various classes of identified root causes.

International events organised by TPF and relating to European research and innovation policies: 
•	“Research to Business” EARTO Annual Conference (Warsaw, 14-15 April 2005)
•	“R&D Management & R&D Communications” Panel Session during the 1st European Innovation Summit, “Strengthening Europe’s Innovation Performance” (Brussels, 13-14 October 2009)
•	“Poland – An Important Driver of the Innovation Union” Debate during the 2nd European Innovation Summit, “Tackling the Grand Challenges – Policy meets Practice” (Brussels, 11-12 October 2010)
•	“Delivering the Innovation Union: The Essential Contribution of RTOs” EARTO Annual Conference (Warsaw, 12-13 May 2011)
•	3rd European Innovation Summit, “Towards a European Innovation Ecosystem” (Warsaw, 10-11 October 2011)
•	“Drivers and Enablers of Innovation - Implementation at Regional Level” Session during the 4th European Innovation Summit, “Building Bridges - Creating Synergies” (Brussels, 9-10 October 2012)
•	“Towards a European Innovation Partnership in Transport” Session during the 5th European Innovation Summit, “Making Innovation Happen” (Brussels, 30 September - 2 October 2013)
•	“Towards a better European Transport System” Session during the 6th European Innovation Summit, “A Mandate for Innovation in Europe” (Brussels, 17-20 November 2014)
•	“Innovation, Internet, Industrialization: An Integrated Highway to Growth” Debate during the 7th European Innovation Summit, “A Pact for Innovation” (Brussels, 7-10 December 2015)
•	“Innovate the European Transport System: Routes to Growth, New Jobs and Reindustrialisation” Debate during the 6th European Innovation Summit, “Future Now!” (Brussels, 14-17 November 2016)
•	“Making the Most of Opportunities in Electromobility” Plenary Session and “Opportunities for innovation and new technology in Electromobility” Debate during the 10th European Innovation Summit, “Europe – A Global Leader in Science, Technology and Innovation” (Brussels, 26-30 November 2018)
raw.goals:
The TECHNOLOGY PARTNERS Foundation (TPF) is a scientific research and implementation organisation with the status of Advanced Technology Centre awarded by the Minister of Science in 2004.

TPF’s mission is to support industry through the transfer of existing and new technologies developed by its partner-institutes, in cooperation with other Polish and foreign research and industrial organisations, with the use of all available national and international instruments. 

TPF’s strategic objective is to initiate and support processes aimed at the rational use of knowledge for innovation activity and to undertake practical actions promoting the development of Polish science and technology cooperation with industry. TPF’s experience includes building partnerships and mapping capabilities, and organising and managing participation in international research programmes.
raw.activity_other: TPF is a founding member of Knowledge4Innovation, (K4I), a platform of the European Parliament whose activities include advocating for making innovation a top priority on the EU's agenda, promoting innovation as the key tool for fostering sustainable economic growth and organising events and debates at the European Parliament (including the annual Innovation Summit) and whose members include 30 MEPs. Dr Kosmider, the Founder and President of the TECHNOLOGY PARTNERS Foundation (2003-2017), was Member of the K4I Management Board and its Vice-President (2015-2017). Currently, Richard Granger, TPF European office Director is K4I's Vice-President.

raw.info_members: The Industry Commons Foundation comprises a Steering Board active in National, European and International policy. Several members are employed by National EU Governments: Martin Curley, Director of Digital Transformation and Open Innovation at HSE Ireland and former Vice President of Intel, head of Intel Labs Europe; Anette Novak, Director of the Swedish Media Council; James Barlow, Professor of Technology and Innovation Management (Healthcare) at Imperial College London; Tobias Edman: Head of Innovation and Public Benefit, Swedish National Space Agency (ESA); Mantalena Kaili, Executive Director of ELONTech–the European Law Observatory of New Technologies; Andrew Dubber, Director of the MTF global innovation prototyping community; Pia Erkinheimo, Director-Private Companies at the Finnish Climate Fund; Bror Salmelin, founder of the European Network of Living Labs and Open Innovation Strategy and Policy Group (OISPG).

raw.activity_relevant_comm:
The foundation continues European policy work of the OISPG (the European Commission's Open Innovation Strategy and Policy Group), whose founders are on the ICF Steering Board, and transfers policy making legacy of Stromatolite LTD (on the Transparency Register since its inception), which was a UK innovation lab closed after 20 years of successful activity due to Brexit.
The foundation's main areas of implementation are:
• The Industry Commons track in the DG RTD Horizon programme
• The first in a series of Industry Commons calls "OntoCommons: ontology driven data documentation for Industry Commons", is a Horizon 2020 funded project which aims to create an ecosystem of translation and reference documentation to enable cross-domain data interoperability. The CSA has gathered great momentum with 19 international partners (several of which are actually clusters of prestigious partner organisations), in addition to 11 industrial demonstrators from the likes of Siemens, Airbus and Bosch, in-kind demonstrators from Japan, Korea, China, and Brazil, and high level US advisors. The Industry Commons Foundation is tasked with ensuring long-term sustainability of the OntoCommons ecosystem after the end of project, including both communities and tools (OCES). The aim is to continue to support and broaden the community, promote results in technology transfer beyond the core community, align with further interoperability developments across industry sectors, and continue to support the evolution of related tools and standards, ensuring results are used as core components of the wider Industry Commons Ecosystem. 
• Cross-domain industry experimental labs

Selected publication areas:
• New European Bauhaus: Magas, M. et al, 2021. New European Bauhaus: Concept paper by the High-Level Roundtable, European Commission.
• Cross-Domain Innovation: Michela Magas & Dimitris Kiritsis, 2021. Industry Commons: an ecosystem approach to horizontal enablers for sustainable cross-domain industrial innovation (a positioning paper), International Journal of Production Research.
• Culture and Creative Sector Policy: Magas, M., 2020. Green Paper: CCI Innovation to Lead Beyond the Pandemic, presented at Framing Creative Futures – The 10th European Creative Industries Summit (ECIS 2020).
• Expansion of EOSC: Magas, M, and Dubber, A., 2020. Expanding EOSC: Engagement of the wider public and private sectors in EOSC, Study funded by the European Union’s Horizon Programme call H2020-INFRAEOSC-2018-4, Grant Agreement number 831644, EOSCsecretariat.eu 2020.
• Advanced manufacturing: Magas, M. in Taisch, M., Casidsid, M.L., May, G., Morin, T.R., Padelli, V., Pinzone, M., Wuest, T. (eds)., 2020. World Manufacturing Report 2020: Manufacturing in the Age of Artificial Intelligence.
• Open Innovation: Curley, M. and Salmelin, B., 2018. Open Innovation 2.0. Springer, Cham
• Digital literacy: Novak, A., 2018. Designing a Renaissance for Digital News Media. Media and Communication, 6(4), pp.115-118.
• Living labs: Salmelin, B. and Dias, A., 2018. Living Labs and Open Innovation in European Context. In Modeling Innovation Sustainability and Technologies (pp. 7-18). Springer, Cham.
• Innovation Recommendations: Magas, M. and Koek, A., Lerhman-Madsen, O., Beetz, K., Curley, M., de Waele, W., Herlitschka, S., 2016. CAF Innovation Recommendations, H2020 Work Programme 2018–2020. DG Connect Advisory Forum, European Commission.
• Horizon 2020 Programme (2016-2018): Magas, M., Fledderus, E. and Herlitschka, S., et al. 2016. CAF's recommendations for H2020's work program 2018–2020. DG Connect Advisory Forum, European Commission.
• Alliance of Internet of Things Innovation: Magas, M., van der Klauw, K. et al., 2015. Report on Analysis and Recommendations for Innovation Ecosystems: The Alliance for Internet of Things Innovation (AIOTI). Published by the European Commission in October 2015.
raw.goals: The Industry Commons Foundation is a not-for-profit foundation established to further and promote the advancement of interdisciplinary knowledge exchange, acquisition of skills, state of the art innovation methodologies and new knowledge creation. Its leadership unites policy makers, industrial science clusters, national bodies, and pan-European Open Innovation experts, and is formally tasked with continuing the activities of the European Commission’s OISPG (Open Innovation Strategy and Policy Group). The leadership builds on extensive experience of authoring Open Innovation methodologies, policy making, research and innovation for large global corporations, and designing knowledge exchange mechanisms for innovator communities of interdisciplinary experts. The foundation actively contributes to design and implementation of supporting structures for transversal collaboration and innovation processes to enable value creation within the broader Industry Commons Ecosystem.

European School of Social Innovation (ESSI)

raw.info_members:
Methods & Skills

In its third decade since foundation, ZSI can offer a broad range of competences which are institutionally firmly embedded. The multi-disciplinary and international team of more than 50 ZSI experts works methodologically sound, innovative in difficult settings as well as tailor-made to meet the demands of our clients. Our methodological know-how is based on quantitative and qualitative empirical social research, but goes beyond the realm of SSH. Innovation research and innovation application, in particular social innovation, are among our unique selling propositions. With our comprehensive regional knowledge and our international networks of stakeholders and partners we have successfully implemented more than 400 research, pilot and transfer projects all over the world.

Please select further keywords on our methods & skills, thematic areas and regions to learn more about our competences!

    Bibliometric Analysis
    Coordination & Project Management
    Data visualisation
    Evaluation & Impact Analysis
    Event Management
    Foresight / Forward-Looking Activities
    Indicator Development
    Innovation Management
    Innovative communication designs & trainings
    Intercultural & Transdisciplinary Cooperation

raw.activity_relevant_comm:
Research – Education – Policy advice – Network coordination

ZSI generates, disseminates, and applies knowledge and skills in thematic areas that relate to the grand societal challenges of our time. In these thematic areas, ZSI acts on different spatial levels from local to global through the deployment of:

Research – design and management of use-inspired research
ZSI staff covers a broad range of use-inspired and use-centred research activities, including trans-disciplinary research, in our core fields of operation. ZSI research projects can be characterised by the following features:
    Collaboration of researchers from different disciplines
    Research projects exhibit different structures and sizes
    For financial support various funding schemes are approached
    Deployment of well-established, innovative methodologies of data collection and analysis
    ZSI research addresses social, ecological, and economic challenges of the 21st century
    Involvement of a wide spectrum of target groups from all societal sectors

Education - design and management of vocational training and academic programmes

The activities of the institute in this area comprise:

    The implementation of SOQUA, a post-graduate academic course for international social-scientific research
    Launching the first academic online training course in Austria on ‘Energy Management and Energy Consulting’ (EMEC starting in 1998)
    Design and establishment of an academic study programme ‘Master of Social Innovation’ (MASI), in collaboration with the Danube University Krems
    Initiation and implementation of the European School of Social Innovation (ESSI)
    30% of ZSI researchers teach at universities, universities of applied sciences, and in other educational organisations
    ZSI’s researchers give at average more than 2.5 presentations per year with a very high international outreach

Policy Advice – strategic intelligence and design, monitoring, evaluation of projects and programmes

ZSI provides consulting services primarily to public entities. ZSI advises on policy planning and implementation of programmes and projects concerning, e.g., demographic change, social exclusion, poverty. We assist ministries in Europe and in world-wide networks in the development of education, RTDI policies and programmes. We offer management support, provide evidence based analysis in support of decision making, and conduct foresight, monitoring, and evaluation. Advisory activities focus on the following services:

    ZSI advises on how to adapt policies to local needs
    ZSI deploys effective mechanisms for organisations to collaborate
    ZSI assists ministries and agencies in planning and delivery of services in policy fields such as labour market, social and educational policy, regional development, science and innovation
    ZSI conducts ex-ante, interim, and ex-post evaluation, as well as monitoring and foresight

ZSI hosts the Austrian Platform for Research and Technology Policy Evaluation, whose members are all relevant federal Austrian ministries engaged in science, technology and innovation policy, all major Austrian RTDI funding organisations, and most importantly, RTDI evaluation providers.

Network coordination – development and implementation of virtual and tangible networks

ZSI initiates and assists the establishment of virtual and real networks, facilitates their implementation. Ntworking projects include knowledge management , multi-lingual communication, inter-cultural sensitivity, tailor-made databases and websites, project management, and financial controlling. Activities in this type of projects include:

    ZSI plays key roles in networks related to social innovation, participatory technology assessment, international science and technology cooperation, and area-based partnerships
    ZSI implements Networks of Excellence, and coordinates networks to bridge inter-linked policy areas
raw.goals:
ZSI – Zentrum für Soziale Innovation (Center for Social Innovation), Vienna
ZSI (www.zsi.at), established 1990 as a private non-profit research organisation, is a pioneer and one of the international thought leaders in social innovation. Legally it is a limited liability company with common public interest status. Since its foundation ZSI aggregated multifaceted experiences in applied social scientific research using both quantitative and qualitative methods. 
ZSI is Austria’s most successful social scientific research institute in EU Framework Programmes (since FP3). Main clients are: EC, ministries, municipalities, OECD, ILO, other public bodies and NGOs. Expertise includes the coordination and implementation of a wide variety of current projects in FP7, Horizon 2020, EU Structural Funds, European Territorial Cooperation, LLP, Erasmus+ and other projects funded nationally and internationally. Since many years, staff of more than 50 experts generates an average annual turnover of about EUR 3.5 mio. ZSI performs project-based agency functions for the Austrian Federal Ministry of Science, Research and Economy (BMWFW) in promotion and support of international STI cooperation.
ZSI’s key activities range from research, education and training, advisory services to public bodies and NGOs, on to networking projects. Thematically they are clustered in three dedicated units: 
o	Work and Equal Opportunities - incl. labour market governance; innovative labour market policies and inclusion; migration research; ageing) 
o	Research Policy and Development - incl. RTDI internationalisation; science-technology-society studies; scientometrics; evaluation) 
o	Technology and Knowledge - incl. participatory technology assessment; technology enhanced learning; science-society dialogue; citizen science)

raw.goals: Advising government organisations, and consortia consisting of government, business, universities and NGOs, in projects about innovation, organizational development, and learning, with a special emphasis on hands-on problem solving in complex social, societal and administrative situations. This work focusses on systemic innovation in the field of public policy-making, sustainable development in civil service organisations, and the development of instruments and methodologies for societal innovation such as action-learning camps for societal innovation and future centers.
